channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump to hold DC rally day before inauguration
item.description: The president-elect has not held a rally in the District of Columbia since he delivered a speech on the Ellipse on Jan. 6, 2021, which preceded a violent attack on the Capitol by supporters attempting to stop the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 10 people are wounded in a shooting outside a New York City nightclub
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ten people were wounded in a shooting outside a New York City nightclub while they were waiting to get into a private event, police said. About 15 people were standing outside Amazura nightclub at 11:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Impeached South Korean president issues defiant message as he faces possible detention
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol, in a message to conservative supporters rallying outside his Seoul residence, vowed to “fight to the end” against “anti-state forces,” while his legal team warned Thursday that police officers attempting to detain him could fa
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bangladesh court again rejects bail for Hindu leader who led rallies
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — A court in southeastern Bangladesh on Thursday rejected a plea for bail by a jailed Hindu leader who led large rallies in the Muslim-majority country demanding better security for minority groups.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden is giving the second highest civilian award to the leaders of the Jan. 6 congressional panel
item.description: President Joe Biden is bestowing the second highest civilian medal on Liz Cheney and Bennie Thompson — the lawmakers who led the congressional investigation into the violent Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol riot by Donald Trump's supporters, and who Trump has said should be jailed.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Army veteran who killed 15 in New Orleans attack was inspired by the Islamic State group
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A U.S. Army veteran who drove a pickup truck into a crowd of New Year’s revelers in New Orleans, killing 15 people, had posted videos to social media hours before the carnage saying he was inspired by the Islamic State group and expressing a desire to kill, the president said.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, JIM MUSTIAN, KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: An aspiring nurse, a football star, a single mother and a father of 2 killed in New Orleans attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — An 18-year-old girl dreaming of becoming a nurse, a single mother, a father of two and a former Princeton football star suffered fatal injuries when the driver of a white pickup truck sped down Bourbon Street, packed with holiday revelers early Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Firework mortars, gas cannisters stuffed inside Tesla that exploded outside Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Firework mortars and camp fuel canisters were found stuffed into the back of the Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside President-elect Donald Trump's Las Vegas hotel early Wednesday, killing a suspect inside the vehicle and sparking an intense investigation into possible terrorism.
item.dc:creator: TY O'NEIL and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rescuers find gruesome scene at a Honolulu home after a fireworks blast kills 3, injures over 20
item.description: Emergency crews arrived to a chaotic and gruesome scene in a Honolulu neighborhood after a large New Year's firework tipped over after being lit and ignited a fiery, shrapnel-studded blast that killed three people and injured more than 20 others, several of them critically.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sugar Bowl CFP quarterfinal between Georgia and Notre Dame postponed after deadly truck attack
item.description: The College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame was postponed a day because of an attack about a mile away from the Superdome early Wednesday, when authorities say a truck driver deliberately plowed into a New Year’s crowd and killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What we know about a vehicle attack on pedestrians in New Orleans that killed at least 15
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Authorities say the driver of a pickup truck sped through a crowd of pedestrians gathered in New Orleans’ bustling French Quarter district early on New Year's Day, killing at least 15 people and injuring about 30 other revelers. The suspect was killed in a shootout with police.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 dead, 7 injured in Tesla fire, explosion at Las Vegas Trump International Hotel on New Year's Day
item.description: A car fire at the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas caused smoke and loud booms, disrupting guests and nearby residents.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jocelyne Wildenstein, Swiss socialite known for extreme plastic surgeries, dies at 84: reports
item.description: Wildenstein's facial surgeries were notably feline, leading the press to (cruelly) dub her "Catwoman.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, NewsNation

item.title: Holiday travel warning: $5M worth of cars stolen from major US airport by organized theft ring, report says
item.description: An organized theft ring is accused of stealing nearly $5 million worth of vehicles from the Dallas-Fort Worth International Airport between 2023 and November 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New California law takes aim at homework burden on students
item.description: You may remember spending hours after school doing homework. A lawmaker in California is hope to change that for the next generation of students in the state.
item.dc:creator: Eytan Wallace, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin police search for convicted felon after multiple children found dead
item.description: Wisconsin authorities found two dead children and a dead adult while searching for a convicted felon who was last seen with a missing child.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Visiting the Big Apple? Prepare to pay more if you drive to busiest part of Manhattan
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York’s much-debated plan to charge drivers to enter the center of Manhattan is set to take effect Sunday, following years of bureaucratic and legal roadblocks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FDA testing aged raw cow's milk cheese for bird flu
item.description: Raw, unpasteurized milk is a known vector of bird flu when derived from infected cow herds. It can also contain pathogens such as E. coli, salmonella, and both Streptococcus and Staphylococcus bacteria.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Neighbors' dispute escalates when Florida man allegedly slashes woman with knife
item.description: Body camera footage shared by a Florida sheriff's office shows a stabbing suspect surrendering to a deputy after an alleged dispute with a neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Outage leaves most of Puerto Rico without power on New Year's Eve
item.description: Nearly 90 percent of 1.47 million clients across Puerto Rico were left in the dark, according to Luma Energy, a private company that oversees electricity transmission and distribution.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Treasury says Chinese hackers remotely accessed workstations, documents in 'major' cyber incident
item.description: The department said it was working with the FBI and the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency and others to investigate the impact of the hack, and that the hack had been attributed to Chinese state-sponsored culprits.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: American family shot on Mexico vacation in attack that left 3 dead, teen fighting for life: reports
item.description: A Chicago teenager vacationing in Mexico is in a coma after he, his father and his uncle became targets of a shooting last week. The father and uncle were killed in the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrant crisis added to record-level US homelessness: HUD
item.description: Sanctuary cities saw homelessness rates jump thanks in part to a surge of migrants being sent from the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, Fox 5

item.title: Angelina Jolie, Brad Pitt reach divorce settlement after 8 years
item.description: Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt have reached a divorce settlement, ending one of the longest and most contentious divorces in Hollywood history but not every legal issue between the two.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: In Memoriam: Those we lost in 2024
item.description: They came from every walk of life to make ours better, richer, more rounded. Here's a look at those we lost in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Australia, New Zealand first to welcome 2025 with fireworks
item.description: The U.S. countdown is still hours away, but those in New Zealand have already started 2025 with a bang.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Census Bureau releases 'population clock' projection for New Year's Day
item.description: Based on current estimates, there will also be one birth every 9 seconds and one death every 9.4 seconds in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, Fox 5

item.title: Gal Gadot says she had 'massive blood clot' in her brain during pregnancy
item.description: "Wonder Woman" actor Gal Gadot revealed Sunday that she underwent emergency surgery earlier this year after a "massive blood clot" was found in her brain while she was eight months pregnant.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Dunkin’ to debut Sabrina Carpenter-inspired espresso drink
item.description: Sabrina Carpenter and Dunkin have been working late throughout the holiday season. On Monday, the breakfast chain announced that Sabrina’s Brown Sugar Shakin’ Espresso, a nod to the Grammy-nominated artist’s chart-topping hit, “Espresso,” will be coming to menus on New Year’s Eve.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Linda Lavin, Tony-winning Broadway actor who starred in TV sitcom ‘Alice,’ dies at 87
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Linda Lavin, a Tony Award-winning stage actor who became a working class icon as a paper-hat wearing waitress on the TV sitcom “Alice,” has died. She was 87.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Tornadoes in Texas and Mississippi kill 2 and injure 6 as severe weather system moves east
item.description: A strong storm system threatened to whip up tornadoes in parts of the U.S. Southeast on Sunday, a day after severe weather claimed at least two lives as twisters touched down in Texas and Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: KTLA 5

item.title: Jayden Daniels dazzles again as Commanders clinch a playoff spot by beating Falcons 30-24 in OT
item.description: Jayden Daniels connected with Zach Ertz in overtime for his third touchdown pass of the game to clinch a playoff spot by beating the Atlanta Falcons 30-24 in overtime Sunday night in a back-and-forth prime-time showdown between highly drafted rookie quarterbacks.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Political world offers condolences for Jimmy Carter
item.description: Members of the political world offered condolences and warm wishes for the family of former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday afternoon. They honored Carter’s decades of achievement and service to the country. Carter, who turned 100 earlier this year, died peacefully in Palins, Ga.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Carter to be honored with events in Georgia, Washington
item.description: Former President Carter will be honored with public observances in both Georgia and Washington, D.C., in the coming days, following his death on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, Fox 5

item.title: US children are struggling with reading
item.description: Learning to read can become exponentially more difficult for older students and adults who have fallen behind, creating grave concerns as the number of individuals struggling with literacy into high school and adulthood is increasing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: After predicting the financial growth of 2024, here's what 2 economists see in 2025
item.description: Brian Blank is a finance scholar and Fed watcher who researches how companies navigate downturns and make financial decisions, as well as how markets process information.
item.dc:creator: WGN 9
